repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes lecture notes, correspondence, and course material on various drama courses. taught.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of English and Comparative Literature, Cornell University from 1956-1991, serving some time as chair in both departments.

He completed his B.A. in English at the University of Buffalo, followed by his M.A. in 1951. In 1956 he received his PhD from Cornell. He received a Fulbright Fellowship at Oxford University in 1954-1955, a senior Fulbright Fellowship, along with a Guggenheim Fellowship to Italy in 1964-1965 and in 1971-1972 he held a National Endowment for the Humanities Fellowship.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes lecture notes, correspondence, and course material on various drama courses. taught.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Anthony Caputi papers, #14/12/3593. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Contemporaries and Followers of Shakespeare to 1642
Correspondence Relating to Masterworks of World Drama
Course in the Methods of Research
History of the English Language / Old English Literature
Literature of the Middle Ages: Religious and Secular (Metrical Romances) to Chaucer
Literature of the Middle Ages: The Period of Great Individual Writers (Langland, Gower, Pearl Poet)
